# Mako Programming Language — Complete Reference for LLMs
#
# Source: https://mako-lang.com
# Repository: https://github.com/loreste/mako
# Version: mako0.1.0
#
# This file contains everything an LLM needs to write correct Mako code.
# Mako compiles .mko files to C, then to native binaries via clang.
# No garbage collector. Memory safety via ownership (hold/share) and arenas.

================================================================================
1. OVERVIEW
================================================================================

Mako is a compiled, statically typed language. Source files use the .mko
extension. The compilation pipeline is: .mko -> C -> .o (cached) -> native
binary. Debug builds use -O0; release builds use -O3 -flto.

Key design points:
- No garbage collector
- Ownership model: hold (move semantics), share (reference counting)
- Arena allocators for scoped bulk allocation
- Structured concurrency via crew blocks (jobs cannot outlive their crew)
- Result and Option types enforced (no null pointers)
- Unused Result values are compile errors
- Batteries-included standard library
- WASM/WASI preview1 target support

Entry point is fn main(). A program is a .mko file with top-level fn, struct,
enum, actor, interface, const, extern "C", and import declarations.

================================================================================
2. INSTALLATION
================================================================================

# From release binary:
curl -fsSL https://github.com/loreste/mako/releases/latest/download/install-release.sh | bash

# From source:
make install    # installs to ~/.local/bin/mako + ~/.local/share/mako/runtime

# Verify:
mako --version    # prints "mako version mako0.1.0 darwin/arm64"

================================================================================
3. BUILD COMMANDS
================================================================================

mako build file.mko                  # debug build (-O0)
mako build --release file.mko        # optimized (-O3 -flto)
mako build file.mko -o mybin         # custom output name
mako run file.mko                    # compile + run
mako run file.mko -- arg1 arg2       # compile + run with arguments
mako check file.mko                  # typecheck only (no binary)
mako check --json file.mko           # JSON diagnostics for tooling
mako test path/                      # run all *_test.mko files
mako test --run TestAdd path/        # filter tests by name
mako test -v path/                   # verbose test output
mako test --count 5 path/            # repeat tests N times
mako test --coverage path/           # with coverage
mako fmt file.mko                    # format to stdout
mako fmt file.mko -w                 # format in place
mako fmt file.mko -d                 # show diff
mako lint path/                      # lint checks
mako bench path/                     # run bench_*.mko files
mako doc path/                       # generate API docs
mako init myapp                      # scaffold a new project
mako init mysvc --backend            # scaffold an API service
mako init myws --workspace           # scaffold a workspace
mako build --target wasm32-wasip1 file.mko -o out.wasm   # WASM target

Build flags:
  --release           Optimized build (-O3 -flto)
  --time              Print compilation timing
  -j N / MAKO_JOBS    Parallel compilation jobs
  --no-incremental    Disable incremental build cache
  --target <triple>   Cross-compile target
  --sanitize=thread   Thread sanitizer
  --sanitize=address  Address sanitizer
  --static-link       Static linking (default for musl targets)
  --emit-c            Emit generated C code

================================================================================
4. TYPES
================================================================================

4.1 Primitive Types
-------------------

  int         Platform integer (int64_t in C backend)
  int64       64-bit signed integer
  int32       32-bit signed integer
  int8        8-bit signed integer (-128..127, range-checked)
  uint64      Unsigned 64-bit integer (rejects negative on convert)
  byte        Unsigned 8-bit (element of []byte)
  float       64-bit floating point (alias: float64)
  bool        true or false
  string      UTF-8 text (byte-indexed, rune-iterable)
  void        No return value

Untyped integer literals default to int. Explicit conversion required between
integer kinds: int64(x), int32(x), int8(x), uint64(x), byte(x), float64(x).

No implicit mixing: int + int64 is a type error. Convert explicitly.

4.2 Composite Types
-------------------

  []T                 Slice (dynamic array): []int, []string, []byte, []float, []Point
  map[K]V             Hash map: map[string]int, map[int]int, map[string]string
  chan[T]              Typed channel: chan[int]
  Option[T]           Some(value) or None
  Result[T, E]        Ok(value) or Err(error)
  Named structs       User-defined product types
  Named enums         User-defined sum types

4.3 Explicit Conversions
------------------------

  let a: int = 10
  let b = int64(a)           // int -> int64
  let f = float64(a)         // int -> float
  print(string(a))           // int -> string "10"
  let buf = bytes("hi")      // string -> []byte (also: []byte("hi"))
  print(string(buf))         // []byte -> string

Compile-time range checking: int8(200) is rejected at mako check when the
argument is a constant.

================================================================================
5. VARIABLES AND BINDINGS
================================================================================

  let x = 1                  // immutable binding, type inferred
  let x: int = 1             // explicit type annotation
  let mut y = 2              // mutable binding
  y = y + 1                  // assignment (= only, never ==)
  const PORT = 8080          // compile-time constant
  const MAX = 10 * 100 + 80  // constant folding supported

Assignment is = only. Equality comparison is ==.

================================================================================
6. OPERATORS
================================================================================

Comparison:   ==  !=  <  >  <=  >=
Arithmetic:   +  -  *  /  %
Logical:      &&  ||  !     (also keywords: and  or  not)
Bitwise:      &  |  ^  &^  <<  >>   (unary ^x = bitwise complement)
String:       +              (concatenation)
String cmp:   ==  !=         (no < > on strings)

Short-circuit: && and || do not evaluate the right side when unnecessary.
!!x is two unary ! operators (no special !! token).

================================================================================
7. FUNCTIONS
================================================================================

  fn greet(name: string) -> string {
      return "hi " + name
  }

  fn add(a: int, b: int) -> int {
      return a + b
  }

  // void return (no -> annotation):
  fn say_hi() {
      print("hi")
  }

  // Trailing expression as return (when no explicit return in body):
  fn area(w: int, h: int) -> int {
      w * h
  }

Missing return on a non-void function is a type error.

Lambdas / closures:

  let double = |x| x * 2
  let ys = fan(xs, |x| x * x)

Lambda syntax: |params| { body } or |params| expr

================================================================================
8. CONTROL FLOW
================================================================================

8.1 If / Else
-------------

  if x > 0 {
      print("positive")
  } else if x == 0 {
      print("zero")
  } else {
      print("negative")
  }

8.2 While Loop
--------------

  let mut i = 0
  while i < 10 {
      i = i + 1
  }

8.3 For / Range
---------------

  let s = [10, 20, 30]

  for i, v in range s {      // index + value
      print_int(i)
      print_int(v)
  }

  for i in range s { }       // indices only
  for _, v in range s { }    // values only (_ is blank)
  for range s { }            // no binders
  for i in range 3 { }       // 0..2 (integer range)
  for i in 3 { }             // legacy: same as range n
  for v in s { }             // legacy: values only

  // Range over strings yields runes (Unicode code points):
  for i, r in range "cafe" {
      // i = byte offset, r = rune (int)
  }

  // Range over map:
  for k, v in range m {      // order unspecified
      print(k)
  }

  // Range over channel:
  for v in range ch {         // recv until close
      print_int(v)
  }

8.4 Break / Continue
--------------------

  break                      // exit innermost loop
  continue                   // next iteration

  // Labeled loops:
  outer: while true {
      while true {
          break outer         // break outer loop
      }
  }

8.5 Defer
---------

  defer print("cleanup")     // runs on function exit (LIFO order)
  defer {
      // block form
      close(fd)
  }

8.6 Match
---------

  // Match on enums:
  match shape {
      Circle(r) => r * r,
      Rect(w, h) => w * h,
      Point => 0,
  }

  // Match on Option:
  match opt {
      Some(v) => v,
      None => fallback,
  }

  // Match on Result:
  match result {
      Ok(v) => v,
      Err(e) => { print(e); -1 },
  }

  // Match on integers:
  match n {
      0 => print("zero"),
      1 => print("one"),
      _ => print("other"),
  }

Match must be exhaustive on enums, Option, and Result.

================================================================================
9. STRUCTS
================================================================================

  struct Point {
      x: int
      y: int
  }

  let mut p = Point { x: 3, y: 4 }
  print_int(p.x)
  p.x = 10

  // Nested structs:
  struct Addr { city: string  zip: int }
  struct Person { name: string  addr: Addr }
  let mut person = Person { name: "Ada", addr: Addr { city: "Paris", zip: 75001 } }
  person.addr.city = "Lyon"

  // Slice of structs:
  let mut xs = make([]Point, 0, 4)
  xs = append(xs, Point { x: 1, y: 2 })

  // Struct methods (free functions with naming convention):
  fn Point_distance(self: Point) -> float {
      sqrt(float64(self.x * self.x + self.y * self.y))
  }
  // Called as: p.distance()

================================================================================
10. ENUMS
================================================================================

  enum Shape {
      Circle(int),
      Rect(int, int),
      Point,
  }

  fn describe(s: Shape) -> int {
      match s {
          Circle(r) => r * r,
          Rect(w, h) => w * h,
          Point => 0,
      }
  }

  // Enum methods:
  fn Shape_area(self: Shape) -> int { ... }
  // Called as: Circle(5).area()

================================================================================
11. INTERFACES
================================================================================

  interface Writer {
      fn write(string) -> int
  }

  // Implementation via naming convention:
  fn Writer_write(s: string) -> int {
      print(s)
      return str_len(s)
  }

  // With self parameter:
  interface Adder {
      fn add(self, delta: int) -> int
  }

  fn Adder_add(self: Counter, delta: int) -> int {
      return self.val + delta
  }
  // Called as: c.add(5) passes c as self

  // Multiple implementations:
  fn Adder_Counter_add(self: Counter, delta: int) -> int { ... }
  fn Adder_Doubler_add(self: Doubler, delta: int) -> int { ... }

  // Dynamic dispatch (interface values box concrete receivers):
  let a: Adder = my_counter    // boxes into fat pointer

================================================================================
12. GENERICS
================================================================================

  // Generic types are supported with angle brackets:
  // Result[T, E], Option[T], List[T]

  fn parse_positive(n: int) -> Result[int, string] {
      if n <= 0 {
          return error("must be positive")
      }
      return Ok(n)
  }

  fn option_or(o: Option[int], fallback: int) -> int {
      match o {
          Some(v) => v,
          None => fallback,
      }
  }

================================================================================
13. ERROR HANDLING
================================================================================

Mako uses Result[T, E] and Option[T] instead of null or exceptions.

13.1 Result Type
----------------

  fn parse(s: string) -> Result[int, string] {
      if str_len(s) == 0 {
          return error("empty input")
      }
      return Ok(42)
  }

  // The ? operator propagates errors (early return on Err):
  fn double_parse(s: string) -> Result[int, string] {
      let v = parse(s)?          // returns Err early if parse fails
      return Ok(v * 2)
  }

  // Match for explicit handling:
  match parse("hello") {
      Ok(v) => print_int(v),
      Err(e) => print(e),
  }

13.2 Error Construction
-----------------------

  error("message")                           // -> Result with Err
  errorf("missing %s", "config.toml")        // formatted error

13.3 Error Wrapping
-------------------

  let r = wrap_err(error("boom"), "parse")   // Err("parse: boom")
  let ok = wrap_err(Ok(7), "unused")         // still Ok(7)

13.4 Error Inspection
---------------------

  error_is(err_result, "substring")          // bool: error contains substring
  error_string(err_result)                   // extract error message string

13.5 Rules
----------

  - Unused Result as a statement is a compile error
  - Use ?, match, or let _ = ... to handle Results
  - Prefer Result + ? + wrap_err over abort for expected failures

================================================================================
14. SLICES
================================================================================

  let mut s = [1, 2, 3]                // []int
  let mut a: []int64 = [10, 20, 30]    // []int64
  let mut b: []byte = [72, 105]        // []byte
  let xs: []string = ["a", "b"]        // []string
  let fs: []float = [1.0, 2.0]         // []float

  // Pre-sized allocation:
  let s = make([]int, 3, 8)            // len 3, cap 8
  let z = make([]byte, 2)              // len 2
  let names = make([]string, 0, 4)     // len 0, cap 4

  // Operations:
  print_int(len(s))                    // length
  print_int(cap(s))                    // capacity
  s = append(s, 4)                     // append (assign result back!)
  let n = copy(dst, src)               // copy elements, returns count

  // Slicing:
  let t = s[1:3]                       // sub-slice (shares backing store)
  let u = s[:3]                        // from start
  let v = s[1:]                        // to end
  let w = s[:]                         // full slice

  // String <-> []byte conversion (copies):
  let buf = bytes("mako")              // string -> []byte
  let buf2 = []byte("mako")            // same as bytes()
  print(string(buf))                   // []byte -> string

  // Type syntax: []T (preferred) or [T]

================================================================================
15. MAPS
================================================================================

  let mut m = make(map[string]int)
  let mut m2 = make(map[string]int, 64)   // with size hint

  m["a"] = 1                           // insert
  print_int(m["a"])                    // get (missing -> zero value)
  print_int(m["missing"])              // 0 (no panic)

  if has(m, "a") { }                   // presence check

  let v, ok = m["a"]                   // comma-ok pattern
  if ok {
      print_int(v)
  }

  delete(m, "a")                       // remove
  print_int(len(m))                    // entry count

  // Iteration (order unspecified):
  for k, v in range m {
      print(k)
      print_int(v)
  }

  // Supported key/value types:
  // map[string]int, map[int]int, map[string]string

================================================================================
16. STRINGS
================================================================================

  let s = "hello"
  let escaped = "hi\tthere\n"         // escapes: \n \t \r \\ \"

  // Concatenation:
  let greeting = "ma" + "ko"

  // Comparison (== and != only, no < > on strings):
  if s == "hello" { }

  // Byte operations:
  print_int(len(s))                   // byte length (5)
  print_int(int(s[0]))               // byte at index
  print(s[1:4])                       // byte substring "ell"

  // Unicode:
  print_int(rune_count("cafe"))       // rune count (4 for "cafe", differs from len for multibyte)

  // Range yields runes:
  for i, r in range "cafe" {
      // i = byte offset, r = rune as int
  }

  // Conversion:
  print(string(42))                   // int -> string "42"
  let b = []byte("Hi!")               // string -> []byte
  print(string(b))                    // []byte -> string

================================================================================
17. OWNERSHIP MODEL
================================================================================

17.1 let / mut
--------------

  let x = 1                           // immutable
  let mut y = 2                       // mutable
  y = y + 1

17.2 hold (Move Semantics)
--------------------------

  hold let x = 7
  hold let y = x                      // x is moved to y
  // print_int(x)                     // ERROR: use of moved value

  // Move into function call:
  hold let x = 42
  print_int(id(x))                    // x moved into id()
  // print_int(x)                     // ERROR: moved

  // Mutable hold:
  hold let mut x = 7
  x = 9                               // reassign before move is OK
  print_int(x)

  // Partial moves (structs):
  hold let p = Point { x: 1, y: 2 }
  let x = p.x                         // moves only x field
  print_int(p.y)                      // y still usable
  // print_int(p.x)                   // ERROR: field moved

  // Copy types (int, float, bool, byte) can be re-read after hold:
  hold let n = 42
  let a = n                           // Copy: n still usable
  print_int(n)                        // OK for Copy types

17.3 share (Reference Counting)
-------------------------------

  hold let a = 1
  share let s = share_int(a)
  print_int(share_get(s))
  share_drop(s)                        // or auto-dropped at block end

  // share let is always immutable (share let mut is rejected)
  // Cannot mutate source while share is live

17.4 arena (Bump Allocation)
----------------------------

  arena a {
      let mut s = make([]int, 3, 8)   // allocated from arena
      s[0] = 10
      s = append(s, 4)                // grows from arena
      // All arena memory freed when block exits
  }

  // Struct arrays in arenas:
  arena a {
      let mut pts = make([]Point, 0, 4)
      pts = append(pts, Point { x: 1, y: 2 })
  }

================================================================================
18. CONCURRENCY
================================================================================

18.1 crew Blocks (Structured Concurrency)
-----------------------------------------

Jobs cannot outlive their crew block:

  crew t {
      let p = t.kick(producer(ch, 5))     // spawn task
      let c = t.kick(consumer(ch))         // spawn task
      let _ = p.join()                     // wait for completion
      print_int(c.join())                  // join returns the result
  }

  // Cancel:
  crew t {
      t.cancel()                          // cooperative cancel
      if t.cancelled() { break }
  }

18.2 fan (Data-Parallel Map)
----------------------------

  let xs = [1, 2, 3, 4]
  let ys = fan(xs, |x| x * x)            // parallel map
  for v in ys {
      print_int(v)
  }

18.3 Channels
-------------

  let ch = chan_new(4)                    // buffered channel, capacity 4
  let _ = ch.send(1)                     // send
  let v = ch.recv()                      // receive
  ch.close()                             // close channel

  // Non-blocking send:
  let ok = chan_try_send(ch, 42)         // 0 on success

  // Channel info:
  print_int(chan_len(ch))                // items in channel
  print_int(chan_cap(ch))                // capacity

  // Range over channel:
  for v in range ch {                    // recv until close
      print_int(v)
  }

18.4 select
-----------

  select timeout 30 {
      a => { print("got a") }
      b => { print("got b") }
      default => { print("default ok") }
  }

  // Value of ready arm:
  let val = chan_select_value()

  // Helpers:
  let which = chan_select2(ch_a, ch_b, timeout_ms)
  let which = chan_select3(ch_a, ch_b, ch_c, timeout_ms)

18.5 Actors
-----------

  actor Session {
      receive Invite { print("invite") }
      receive Timer { print("tick") }
      receive Bye { print("bye") }
  }

  fn main() {
      let session = Session_spawn()
      crew t {
          let loopj = t.kick(Session_loop(session))
          let _ = Session_send(session, Session_Invite())
          let _ = Session_send(session, Session_Bye())
          print_int(loopj.join())
      }
  }

18.6 Concurrent Maps (CMap)
---------------------------

Thread-safe hash map, safe to share across crew tasks:

  let m = cmap_new()
  cmap_set(m, "hello", "world")
  print(cmap_get(m, "hello"))            // "world"
  print_int(cmap_has(m, "hello"))        // 1
  print_int(cmap_len(m))                 // 1
  let n = cmap_incr(m, "counter", 5)     // atomic increment
  print_int(cmap_del(m, "hello"))        // 1 (existed)

================================================================================
19. IMPORT SYSTEM
================================================================================

19.1 Local File Import
----------------------

  import "./my_lib.mko"                  // flat merge
  print_int(lib_add(2, 3))              // call functions from imported file

19.2 Aliased Import
-------------------

  import "./my_lib.mko" as lib
  print_int(lib.add(10, 32))            // namespaced call

19.3 Standard Library Packages
------------------------------

  import "strings"
  import "sync"
  assert(strings.contains("hi", "h"))
  let m = sync.rwmutex()

19.4 Grouped Import
-------------------

  import (
      "strings"
      "path"
      lib "./my_lib.mko"
  )

  // Brace form also accepted:
  import { "fmt"; "strings" }

19.5 Package Dependencies (mako.toml)
-------------------------------------

  # mako.toml
  [package]
  name = "myapp"
  version = "0.1.0"

  [dependencies]
  "helper" = { path = "../helper", version = "0.1.0" }
  "tool" = { git = "https://example.com/tool.git", tag = "v0.1.0" }

  # Commands:
  mako pkg add helper ../helper          # add path dependency
  mako pkg fetch                         # clone git deps
  mako pkg list                          # list dependencies
  mako pkg lock                          # pin hashes in mako.lock
  mako pkg audit                         # advisory + license check

  # Usage: symbols namespaced by dependency key
  // In code: helper.add(1, 2)

================================================================================
20. TESTING
================================================================================

  // Test file: add_test.mko (same directory as code)
  // Test functions start with "Test":

  fn TestAdd() {
      assert_eq(add(2, 3), 5)
  }

  fn TestAddTable() {
      let a = [1, 2, 10]
      let b = [1, 3, 5]
      let want = [2, 5, 15]
      for i in 3 {
          assert_eq(add(a[i], b[i]), want[i])
      }
  }

  // Subtests:
  fn TestSubs() {
      t_run("case_one")
      assert_eq(1, 1)
      t_run("case_two")
      assert_eq(2, 2)
  }

  // Nested subtests:
  fn TestNested() {
      t_run("outer")
      t_run_nested("inner")     // prints TestNested/outer/inner

  }

  // Test categories:
  fn FuzzXxx() { ... }
  fn PropertyXxx() { ... }

  // Run tests:
  mako test path/
  mako test --run TestAdd path/
  mako test -v path/                     // verbose
  mako test --run '/^TestAdd$/' path/    // regex filter
  mako test --count 5 path/              // repeat

================================================================================
21. JSON AND DERIVE
================================================================================

21.1 derive(json)
-----------------

  #[derive(json)]
  struct Person {
      name: string
      age: int
  }

  fn main() {
      let j = Person_to_json("Ada", 36)
      let name = Person_name_from_json(j)
      let age = Person_age_from_json(j)
  }

21.2 JSON Functions
-------------------

  // Construction:
  json_object("key", "value")                           // {"key":"value"}
  json_ss("k1", "v1", "k2", "v2")                      // {"k1":"v1","k2":"v2"}
  json_si("name", "Ada", "age", 36)                     // {"name":"Ada","age":36}
  json_i("count", 42)                                   // {"count":42}
  json_nest("addr", addr_json)                          // nest under key
  json_merge(obj1, obj2)                                // merge two objects
  json_object_from_map_ss(m)                            // map[string]string -> JSON

  // Extraction:
  json_get_string(json, "key")                          // extract string
  json_get_int(json, "key")                             // extract int
  json_get_object(json, "key")                          // extract nested object

  // Path navigation:
  json_path_string(json, "addr", "city")                // dot-path string
  json_path_int(json, "data", "count")                  // dot-path int

  // Arrays:
  json_array_ints3(10, 20, 30)                          // [10,20,30]
  json_array_strings2("a", "b")                         // ["a","b"]
  json_array_push_int(arr, 40)                          // append int
  json_array_push_string(arr, "c")                      // append string
  json_array_len(json)                                  // array length
  json_array_get_int(json, 0)                           // get int at index
  json_array_get_string(json, 0)                        // get string at index

  // Checking:
  json_has(json, "key", "value")                        // check key=value

================================================================================
22. EXTERN C (FFI)
================================================================================

  extern "C" fn mako_c_abs(n: int) -> int
  extern "C" fn mako_c_add(a: int, b: int) -> int

  fn main() {
      print_int(mako_c_abs(0 - 42))     // 42
      print_int(mako_c_add(20, 22))     // 42
  }

================================================================================
23. WASM / WASI
================================================================================

  # Build for WASI (requires wasi-sdk, set WASI_SDK_PATH):
  mako build file.mko --target wasm32-wasip1 -o out.wasm

  # Run with wasmtime:
  wasmtime out.wasm
  wasmtime --env KEY=val out.wasm arg1 arg2
  wasmtime --dir=./sandbox::. out.wasm    # filesystem access

  # Available in WASI: print, fib, argc, arg_get, env_get,
  # read_file, write_file (with --dir preopens)
  # Not available in WASI: sockets, TLS, databases

  # Browser/edge deployment:
  mako deploy wasm dist/ --entry file.mko --wasm app.wasm

================================================================================
24. STANDARD LIBRARY REFERENCE
================================================================================

Below are the most important built-in functions organized by category.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.1 Output
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  print(s: string) -> void                    Print string to stdout
  print_int(n: int) -> void                   Print integer to stdout
  print_int64(n: int64) -> void               Print int64
  print_int32(n: int32) -> void               Print int32
  print_int8(n: int8) -> void                 Print int8
  print_uint64(n: uint64) -> void             Print uint64
  print_float(f: float) -> void               Print float
  print_bool(b: bool) -> void                 Print bool
  dbg(n: int) -> int                          Debug-print int, return it
  dbg_str(s: string) -> string                Debug-print string, return it
  format_int(n: int) -> string                Int to string
  format_float(f: float, prec: int) -> string Float to string with precision
  format_bool(b: bool) -> string              Bool to "true"/"false"
  int_to_string(n: int) -> string             Int to string
  fmt_sprintf(fmt: string, arg: string) -> string    Sprintf with string arg
  fmt_sprintf_d(fmt: string, arg: int) -> string     Sprintf with int arg

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.2 Strings
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  str_len(s: string) -> int                   Byte length
  str_eq(a: string, b: string) -> bool        Equality test
  str_contains(s: string, sub: string) -> bool  Contains substring
  str_has_prefix(s: string, pfx: string) -> bool  Starts with
  str_has_suffix(s: string, sfx: string) -> bool  Ends with
  str_index(s: string, sub: string) -> int    First occurrence index, -1 if not found
  str_last_index(s: string, sub: string) -> int  Last occurrence
  str_trim(s: string, cutset: string) -> string  Trim chars from both ends
  str_trim_space(s: string) -> string         Trim whitespace
  str_trim_left(s: string, cutset: string) -> string
  str_trim_right(s: string, cutset: string) -> string
  str_to_lower(s: string) -> string           Lowercase
  str_to_upper(s: string) -> string           Uppercase
  str_repeat(s: string, n: int) -> string     Repeat n times
  str_replace(s: string, old: string, new: string) -> string  Replace all
  str_split(s: string, sep: string) -> []string  Split by separator
  str_fields(s: string) -> []string           Split on whitespace
  str_join(parts: []string, sep: string) -> string  Join with separator
  str_count(s: string, sub: string) -> int    Count occurrences
  str_cut(s: string, sep: string) -> []string Cut at first sep -> [before, after]
  rune_count(s: string) -> int                Unicode code point count

  // String builder (growable buffer):
  str_builder() -> StrBuilder                 Create builder
  builder_write(sb: StrBuilder, s: string) -> void  Append string
  builder_write_byte(sb: StrBuilder, b: byte) -> void  Append byte
  builder_string(sb: StrBuilder) -> string    Finalize to string
  builder_len(sb: StrBuilder) -> int          Current length

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.3 Math
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  abs(n: int) -> int                          Absolute value (int)
  abs_f(f: float) -> float                    Absolute value (float)
  min(a: int, b: int) -> int                  Minimum
  max(a: int, b: int) -> int                  Maximum
  clamp(val: int, lo: int, hi: int) -> int    Clamp to range
  clamp_f(val: float, lo: float, hi: float) -> float
  sqrt(f: float) -> float                     Square root
  sin(f: float) -> float                      Sine (radians)
  cos(f: float) -> float                      Cosine (radians)
  atan2(y: float, x: float) -> float          Arctangent
  floor_f(f: float) -> float                  Floor
  ceil_f(f: float) -> float                   Ceiling
  lerp(a: float, b: float, t: float) -> float Linear interpolation
  dist2d(x1: float, y1: float, x2: float, y2: float) -> float  2D distance
  math_pow(base: float, exp: float) -> float  Power
  math_log(f: float) -> float                 Natural log
  math_exp(f: float) -> float                 Exponential
  rand_seed(seed: int) -> void                Seed RNG
  rand_intn(n: int) -> int                    Random int in [0, n)
  rand_float() -> float                       Random float in [0.0, 1.0)
  random_int(lo: int, hi: int) -> int         Crypto-random int in [lo, hi]
  safe_add(a: int, b: int) -> int             Overflow-checked addition

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.4 Parsing & Conversion
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  parse_int(s: string) -> Result[int, string]    Parse string as int
  parse_float(s: string) -> float                Parse string as float
  parse_bool(s: string) -> Result[int, string]   Parse string as bool (1/0)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.5 Slices & Arrays
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  len(arr: []T) -> int                        Length
  cap(arr: []T) -> int                        Capacity
  append(arr: []T, val: T) -> []T             Append, return new slice
  copy(dst: []T, src: []T) -> int             Copy elements, return count
  ints_contains(arr: []int, val: int) -> bool  Check membership
  strings_contains(arr: []string, val: string) -> bool
  ints_index(arr: []int, val: int) -> int     Index of value, -1 if missing
  ints_copy(arr: []int) -> []int              Shallow copy
  sort_ints(arr: []int) -> []int              Sort ascending
  sort_strings(arr: []string) -> []string     Sort ascending
  slices_reverse(arr: []int) -> []int         Reverse
  slices_unique(arr: []int) -> []int          Remove duplicates

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.6 Maps
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  has(m: map[K]V, key: K) -> bool             Key presence check
  delete(m: map[K]V, key: K)                  Remove entry
  maps_keys(m: map[string]int) -> []string    All keys
  maps_values(m: map[string]int) -> []int     All values
  maps_clear(m: map[string]int) -> void       Remove all
  maps_clone(m: map[string]int) -> map[string]int  Shallow copy
  maps_equal(a: map[string]int, b: map[string]int) -> int
  maps_copy(dst: map[string]int, src: map[string]int) -> void

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.7 File I/O
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  read_file(path: string) -> string           Read entire file
  write_file(path: string, data: string) -> int  Write/overwrite file
  append_file(path: string, data: string) -> int  Append to file
  remove_file(path: string) -> int            Delete file
  file_exists(path: string) -> bool           Check existence
  is_dir(path: string) -> bool                Check if directory
  read_dir(path: string) -> []string          List directory entries
  mkdir(path: string) -> int                  Create directory
  getcwd() -> string                          Current working directory
  chdir(path: string) -> int                  Change directory
  path_join(a: string, b: string) -> string   Join path segments
  path_base(path: string) -> string           Last element of path
  path_dir(path: string) -> string            Directory portion
  path_ext(path: string) -> string            File extension
  path_clean(path: string) -> string          Normalize path
  path_is_abs(path: string) -> bool           Check if absolute
  filepath_walk(root: string) -> []string     Recursive file listing
  embed_file(path: string) -> string          Embed file at compile time

  // Direct I/O (low-level):
  file_open(path: string, flags: int, mode: int) -> int  Open fd (0=read,1=write,2=rw)
  file_close(fd: int) -> int                  Close fd
  pread(fd: int, count: int, offset: int) -> string  Positional read
  pwrite(fd: int, data: string, offset: int) -> int  Positional write
  fsync(fd: int) -> int                       Flush data + metadata
  fdatasync(fd: int) -> int                   Flush data only
  fallocate(fd: int, size: int) -> int        Pre-allocate space
  file_size(fd: int) -> int                   Size of open file
  file_truncate(fd: int, size: int) -> int    Truncate/extend

  // Memory-mapped files:
  mmap_open(path: string, size: int) -> MMap     Map existing file
  mmap_create(path: string, size: int) -> MMap   Create + map new file
  mmap_read(m: MMap, offset: int, len: int) -> string
  mmap_write(m: MMap, offset: int, data: string) -> int
  mmap_sync(m: MMap, flags: int) -> int          Flush to disk
  mmap_size(m: MMap) -> int
  mmap_close(m: MMap) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.8 Environment & CLI
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  env_get(key: string) -> string              Get env var
  env_set(key: string, val: string) -> int    Set env var
  env_get_or(key: string, fallback: string) -> string  Get with default
  env_has(key: string) -> int                 Check if set
  argc() -> int                               Argument count
  args() -> []string                          All arguments
  arg_get(idx: int) -> string                 Argument by index
  flag_string(name: string, default: string) -> string  Parse string flag
  flag_int(name: string, default: int) -> int Parse int flag
  flag_bool(name: string, default: int) -> int Parse bool flag

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.9 Time
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  now_ns() -> int                             Nanoseconds since epoch
  now_ms() -> int                             Milliseconds since epoch
  time_unix() -> int                          Unix timestamp (seconds)
  time_format(unix: int) -> string            Format as RFC3339 UTC
  sleep_ms(ms: int) -> void                   Sleep milliseconds
  time_sleep_ms(ms: int) -> void              Alias for sleep_ms
  elapsed_ms(start: int) -> int               Ms elapsed since start

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.10 HTTP Server
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  http_bind(port: int) -> int                 Bind listener on port
  http_accept(listener: int) -> int           Accept connection -> conn id
  http_method(conn: int) -> string            Request method
  http_path(conn: int) -> string              Request path
  http_body(conn: int) -> string              Request body
  http_header(conn: int, name: string) -> string  Request header
  http_respond(conn: int, status: int, body: string) -> int
  http_respond_json(conn: int, status: int, json: string) -> int
  http_respond_ct(conn: int, status: int, body: string, ct: string) -> int
  http_close(conn: int) -> int                Close connection
  http_close_listener(listener: int) -> int   Close listener
  http_next(conn: int) -> int                 Next request (keep-alive)
  http_keepalive(conn: int) -> int            Check keep-alive support

  // Request parsing:
  http_request_parse(raw: string) -> HttpRequest
  http_route_match(req: HttpRequest, method: string, pattern: string) -> bool
  http_route_param(req: HttpRequest, pattern: string, name: string) -> string

  // Graceful shutdown:
  http_shutdown_begin(timeout: int) -> int
  http_shutdown_requested() -> int
  http_shutdown_ready() -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.11 HTTP Client
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  http_get(url: string) -> string             GET request
  http_post(url: string, body: string) -> string  POST request
  http_request(method: string, url: string, body: string, timeout: int) -> string
  http_get_timeout(url: string, ms: int) -> string
  http_post_timeout(url: string, body: string, ms: int) -> string
  http_last_status() -> int                   Status of last response
  http_last_header(name: string) -> string    Header from last response

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.12 JSON (see also section 21)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  json_object(key: string, value: string) -> string
  json_get_string(json: string, key: string) -> string
  json_get_int(json: string, key: string) -> int
  json_get_object(json: string, key: string) -> string
  json_has(json: string, key: string, value: string) -> int
  json_nest(key: string, inner: string) -> string
  json_merge(a: string, b: string) -> string
  json_ss(k1: string, v1: string, k2: string, v2: string) -> string
  json_si(k1: string, v1: string, k2: string, v2: int) -> string
  json_i(key: string, value: int) -> string
  json_object_from_map_ss(m: map[string]string) -> string
  json_path_string(json: string, path: string, key: string) -> string
  json_path_int(json: string, path: string, key: string) -> int
  json_array_len(json: string) -> int
  json_array_get_int(json: string, idx: int) -> int
  json_array_get_string(json: string, idx: int) -> string
  json_array_push_string(json: string, val: string) -> string
  json_array_push_int(json: string, val: int) -> string
  json_array_ints3(a: int, b: int, c: int) -> string
  json_array_strings2(a: string, b: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.13 Database
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Unified SQL (SQLite + PostgreSQL):
  sql_open_sqlite(path: string) -> SqlDB
  sql_open_postgres(connstr: string) -> SqlDB
  sql_ok(db: SqlDB) -> int                   Check connection valid
  sql_close(db: SqlDB) -> int                Close connection
  sql_exec(db: SqlDB, sql: string, params: []int) -> int  Execute with int params
  sql_exec_plain(db: SqlDB, sql: string) -> int  Execute without params
  sql_exec_str4(db: SqlDB, sql: string, a: string, b: string, c: string, d: string) -> int
  sql_query_int(db: SqlDB, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_query_str(db: SqlDB, sql: string, param: string) -> string
  sql_begin(db: SqlDB) -> int                Begin transaction
  sql_commit(db: SqlDB) -> int               Commit
  sql_rollback(db: SqlDB) -> int             Rollback
  sql_prepare(db: SqlDB, sql: string) -> int Prepare statement
  sql_migrate(db: SqlDB, version: int, sql: string) -> int  Apply migration

  // Connection pool:
  sql_pool_open_sqlite(path: string, size: int) -> int
  sql_pool_open_postgres(connstr: string, size: int) -> int
  sql_pool_query_int(pool: int, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_pool_exec(pool: int, sql: string, params: []int) -> int
  sql_pool_close(pool: int) -> int

  // Legacy SQLite:
  sqlite_query_int(db: string, sql: string) -> int
  sqlite_query_text(db: string, sql: string) -> string

  // PostgreSQL (libpq):
  pg_connect(connstr: string) -> PgConn
  pg_exec(conn: PgConn, sql: string) -> int
  pg_close(conn: PgConn) -> int

  // MySQL:
  mysql_connect(connstr: string) -> MysqlConn
  mysql_ok(conn: MysqlConn) -> int
  mysql_close(conn: MysqlConn) -> int

  // Redis:
  redis_connect(addr: string) -> RedisConn
  redis_conn_set(conn: RedisConn, key: string, val: string) -> string
  redis_conn_get(conn: RedisConn, key: string) -> string
  redis_conn_del(conn: RedisConn, key: string) -> string
  redis_close(conn: RedisConn) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.14 Crypto & Security
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  sha1(data: string) -> string               SHA-1 hash (raw bytes)
  sha256(data: string) -> string             SHA-256 hash (raw bytes)
  sha512(data: string) -> string             SHA-512 hash (raw bytes)
  hmac_sha256(key: string, data: string) -> string  HMAC-SHA256 (raw bytes)
  random_bytes(n: int) -> string             Crypto-random bytes
  const_eq(a: string, b: string) -> int      Constant-time comparison
  crypto_eq(a: string, b: string) -> int     Constant-time byte comparison
  secret_from_str(s: string) -> Secret       Wrap as secret (zeroized on drop)
  secret_drop(s: Secret) -> void             Securely erase
  aes_gcm_seal(key: string, nonce: string, pt: string, aad: string) -> string
  aes_gcm_open(key: string, nonce: string, ct: string, aad: string) -> string
  chacha20_poly1305_seal(key: string, nonce: string, pt: string, aad: string) -> string
  chacha20_poly1305_open(key: string, nonce: string, ct: string, aad: string) -> string

  // Hex helpers (use with sha256/hmac_sha256):
  // sha256_hex, hmac_sha256_hex available via hex_encode(sha256(data))

  // JWT:
  jwt_sign(payload: string, secret: string) -> string
  jwt_verify(token: string, secret: string) -> int
  jwt_payload(token: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.15 Encoding
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  base64_encode(data: string) -> string
  base64_decode(data: string) -> string
  base32_encode(data: string) -> string
  hex_encode(data: string) -> string
  hex_decode(data: string) -> string
  url_query_escape(s: string) -> string
  gzip_compress(data: string) -> string
  gzip_decompress(data: string) -> string

  // Binary encoding:
  binary_put_u16le(n: int) -> string          Little-endian uint16
  binary_put_u32le(n: int) -> string          Little-endian uint32
  binary_put_u64le(n: int) -> string          Little-endian uint64
  binary_u16le(data: string) -> int           Decode LE uint16
  binary_u32le(data: string) -> int           Decode LE uint32
  binary_put_u16be(n: int) -> string          Big-endian uint16
  binary_put_u32be(n: int) -> string          Big-endian uint32
  binary_u16be(data: string) -> int           Decode BE uint16
  binary_u32be(data: string) -> int           Decode BE uint32

  // Serialization:
  yaml_get_string(yaml: string, key: string) -> string
  toml_get_string(toml: string, key: string) -> string
  toml_get_int(toml: string, key: string) -> int
  csv_split_line(line: string) -> []string
  csv_join_row(fields: []string) -> string
  xml_escape(s: string) -> string
  html_escape(s: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.16 Regular Expressions
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  regex_match(pattern: string, s: string) -> bool      Full match
  regex_find(pattern: string, s: string) -> string      First match
  regex_find_all(pattern: string, s: string, max: int) -> []string
  regex_replace(pattern: string, s: string, repl: string) -> string  First
  regex_replace_all(pattern: string, s: string, repl: string) -> string
  regex_capture(pattern: string, s: string, group: int) -> string
  regex_valid(pattern: string) -> int         Check if pattern is valid
  regex_quote_meta(s: string) -> string       Escape metacharacters

  // Supported: literals, ., X*, X+, X?, |, [abc], [a-z], [^...], (...), ^, $

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.17 UUID
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  uuid_v4() -> Uuid                           Random v4 UUID
  uuid_string(u: Uuid) -> string              To "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-..."
  uuid_parse(s: string) -> Uuid               Parse string (nil on failure)
  uuid_parse_ok(s: string) -> bool            Validate UUID string
  uuid_eq(a: Uuid, b: Uuid) -> bool           Compare
  uuid_is_nil(u: Uuid) -> bool                Check nil
  uuid_nil() -> Uuid                          Nil UUID
  uuid_check(s: string) -> Result[int, string]  Parse and validate

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.18 Cookies, Sessions & Auth
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Cookies:
  cookie_get(header: string, name: string) -> string
  cookie_make(name: string, value: string, max_age: int) -> string

  // Sessions:
  session_id_new() -> string                  32-char random hex session ID

  // CSRF:
  csrf_token() -> string                      Generate CSRF token
  csrf_check(token: string, expected: string) -> int  Constant-time check

  // Bearer auth:
  auth_bearer(header: string) -> string       Extract bearer token
  auth_check_bearer(header: string, expected: string) -> int

  // Basic auth:
  auth_basic_header(user: string, pass: string) -> string
  auth_check_basic(header: string, user: string, pass: string) -> int

  // Signed tokens (HMAC-SHA256):
  auth_token_sign(subject: string, secret: string) -> string
  auth_token_check(token: string, secret: string) -> int
  auth_token_subject(token: string) -> string

  // Session cookie:
  auth_session_cookie(cookie_hdr: string, name: string, expected: string) -> int

  // Role-based access:
  auth_role_has(roles: string, role: string) -> int
  authz_allow_role(user_roles: string, required: string) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.19 Channels
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  chan_new(capacity: int) -> chan[int]         Create buffered channel
  chan_try_send(ch: chan[int], val: int) -> int  Non-blocking send (0=ok)
  chan_len(ch: chan[int]) -> int               Items in channel
  chan_cap(ch: chan[int]) -> int               Channel capacity
  chan_select2(a: chan[int], b: chan[int], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_select3(a: chan[int], b: chan[int], c: chan[int], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_select4(a: chan[int], b: chan[int], c: chan[int], d: chan[int], timeout_ms: int) -> int
  chan_select_value() -> int                  Value from last select

  // Channel methods:
  ch.send(val)                                // send
  ch.recv()                                   // receive
  ch.close()                                  // close

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.20 Concurrency Primitives
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Mutex:
  mutex_new() -> Mutex
  mutex_lock(m: Mutex) -> void
  mutex_unlock(m: Mutex) -> void

  // RWMutex:
  rwmutex_new() -> RWMutex
  rwmutex_rlock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_runlock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_lock(m: RWMutex) -> void
  rwmutex_unlock(m: RWMutex) -> void

  // Atomics:
  atomic_new(val: int) -> AtomicInt
  atomic_load(a: AtomicInt) -> int
  atomic_store(a: AtomicInt, val: int) -> void
  atomic_add(a: AtomicInt, delta: int) -> int
  atomic_cas(a: AtomicInt, expected: int, desired: int) -> int

  // WaitGroup:
  wait_group_new() -> WaitGroup
  wait_group_add(wg: WaitGroup, delta: int) -> void
  wait_group_done(wg: WaitGroup) -> void
  wait_group_wait(wg: WaitGroup) -> void

  // Shared references:
  share_int(val: int) -> ShareInt
  share_clone(s: ShareInt) -> ShareInt
  share_get(s: ShareInt) -> int
  share_drop(s: ShareInt) -> void

  // Concurrent map:
  cmap_new() -> CMap
  cmap_set(m: CMap, key: string, val: string) -> void
  cmap_get(m: CMap, key: string) -> string
  cmap_has(m: CMap, key: string) -> int
  cmap_del(m: CMap, key: string) -> int
  cmap_len(m: CMap) -> int
  cmap_incr(m: CMap, key: string, delta: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.21 Event Loop
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  evloop_new() -> EvLoop                      Create event loop
  evloop_add(el: EvLoop, fd: int, flags: int) -> int  Monitor fd
  evloop_mod(el: EvLoop, fd: int, flags: int) -> int  Modify interest
  evloop_del(el: EvLoop, fd: int) -> int      Remove fd
  evloop_wait(el: EvLoop, timeout_ms: int) -> int  Wait, returns ready count
  evloop_event_fd(el: EvLoop, idx: int) -> int  Ready fd at index
  evloop_event_flags(el: EvLoop, idx: int) -> int  Ready flags at index
  evloop_close(el: EvLoop) -> int             Destroy event loop

  // Non-blocking I/O:
  nb_listen(port: int) -> int                 Non-blocking TCP listener
  nb_accept(fd: int) -> int                   Non-blocking accept
  nb_read(fd: int) -> string                  Non-blocking read
  nb_write(fd: int, data: string) -> int      Non-blocking write
  nb_udp_bind(port: int) -> int               Non-blocking UDP
  nb_udp_recv(fd: int) -> string              Non-blocking UDP recv
  nb_close(fd: int) -> int                    Close

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.22 Binary Buffer
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  buf_pack_new(capacity: int) -> Buf          Create write buffer
  buf_from_string(s: string) -> Buf           Buffer from string
  buf_to_string(b: Buf) -> string             Extract as string
  buf_len(b: Buf) -> int                      Total length
  buf_pos(b: Buf) -> int                      Current position
  buf_reset(b: Buf) -> void                   Reset to start
  buf_seek(b: Buf, pos: int) -> void          Seek to position
  buf_free(b: Buf) -> void                    Free memory

  // Write (little-endian unless noted):
  buf_write_u8(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u16(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u32(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_u64(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_i32(b: Buf, val: int) -> void
  buf_write_f32(b: Buf, val: float) -> void
  buf_write_f64(b: Buf, val: float) -> void
  buf_write_bytes(b: Buf, data: string) -> void
  buf_write_str(b: Buf, s: string) -> void
  buf_write_u16be(b: Buf, val: int) -> void   Big-endian
  buf_write_u32be(b: Buf, val: int) -> void   Big-endian

  // Read:
  buf_read_u8(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u16(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u32(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_u64(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_i32(b: Buf) -> int
  buf_read_f32(b: Buf) -> float
  buf_read_f64(b: Buf) -> float
  buf_read_bytes(b: Buf, n: int) -> string
  buf_read_str(b: Buf) -> string
  buf_read_u16be(b: Buf) -> int               Big-endian
  buf_read_u32be(b: Buf) -> int               Big-endian

  // Byte buffer pool:
  buf_get(size: int) -> []byte                Get from pool
  buf_put(b: []byte) -> void                  Return to pool

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.23 Game Networking
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  game_udp_bind(port: int) -> GameUDP         Bind game UDP socket
  game_udp_recv(sock: GameUDP) -> string      Receive packet
  game_udp_sender(sock: GameUDP) -> int       Sender peer ID
  game_udp_send(sock: GameUDP, peer: int, data: string) -> int
  game_udp_broadcast(sock: GameUDP, data: string) -> int
  game_udp_kick(sock: GameUDP, peer: int) -> void
  game_udp_peers(sock: GameUDP) -> int        Connected peer count
  game_udp_fd(sock: GameUDP) -> int           Raw fd for event loop
  game_udp_close(sock: GameUDP) -> void
  tick_now_us() -> int                        Microsecond timestamp
  tick_sleep_us(target: int, period: int) -> int  Sleep to maintain tick rate

  // Fixed timestep:
  game_fixed_steps(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int
  game_fixed_remainder(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int
  game_alpha(dt: int, step: int, accum: int) -> int

  // Fixed-point math:
  fx_from_int(val: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_to_int(val: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_mul(a: int, b: int, shift: int) -> int
  fx_div(a: int, b: int, shift: int) -> int

  // Deterministic RNG:
  det_rng_next(state: int) -> int
  det_rng_range(state: int, max: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.24 Cloud / Distributed
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // Consistent hashing:
  chash_new(replicas: int, nodes: int) -> CHash
  chash_get(ring: CHash, key: string) -> int
  chash_add_node(ring: CHash) -> int
  chash_remove_node(ring: CHash, node: int) -> void
  chash_node_count(ring: CHash) -> int
  chash_free(ring: CHash) -> void

  // Rate limiting:
  ratelimit_new(max: int, window_ms: int) -> RateLimiter
  ratelimit_allow(rl: RateLimiter) -> int
  ratelimit_remaining(rl: RateLimiter) -> int
  ratelimit_free(rl: RateLimiter) -> void
  rate_allow(key: string, max: int, window_ms: int) -> int

  // Circuit breaker:
  breaker_new(threshold: int, timeout_ms: int, half_max: int) -> CircuitBreaker
  breaker_allow(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> int
  breaker_success(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_failure(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_state(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> int    // 0=closed, 1=open, 2=half-open
  breaker_reset(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void
  breaker_free(cb: CircuitBreaker) -> void

  // Backoff:
  backoff_ms(attempt: int, base_ms: int, max_ms: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.25 TCP & UDP Networking
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  // TCP:
  tcp_listen(port: int) -> int                Listen on port
  tcp_accept(listener: int) -> int            Accept connection
  tcp_connect(host: string, port: int) -> int Connect to server
  tcp_write(conn: int, data: string) -> int   Write data
  tcp_read(conn: int) -> string               Read data
  tcp_nodelay(conn: int) -> int               Set TCP_NODELAY
  tcp_close(conn: int) -> int                 Close connection

  // UDP:
  udp_bind(port: int) -> int                  Bind UDP socket
  udp_send_to(fd: int, host: string, port: int, data: string) -> int
  udp_recv(fd: int, max_bytes: int) -> string
  udp_close(fd: int) -> int

  // Unix sockets:
  unix_socket_pair() -> int
  unix_write(fd: int, data: string) -> int
  unix_read(fd: int, max: int) -> string
  unix_close(fd: int) -> int

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.26 TLS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  tls_serve(port: int, cert: string, key: string, handler: string) -> int
  tls_serve_once(port: int, cert: string, key: string, response: string) -> int
  tls_serve_n(port: int, cert: string, key: string, response: string, n: int) -> int
  tls_get(host: string, port: int, path: string, ca: string) -> string
  tls_post(host: string, port: int, path: string, ca: string, body: string) -> string
  tls_get_insecure(host: string, port: int, path: string) -> string
  tls_handshake_ok(host: string, port: int, ca: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.27 Testing
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  assert(cond: bool) -> void                  Assert true
  assert_eq(a: int, b: int) -> void           Assert ints equal
  assert_eq_str(a: string, b: string) -> void Assert strings equal
  fail(msg: string) -> void                   Unconditional failure
  t_run(name: string) -> void                 Named subtest
  t_run_nested(name: string) -> void           Nested subtest
  black_box(n: int) -> int                    Prevent optimization (benchmarks)

  // HTTP test helpers:
  httptest_serve_once(port: int, response: string) -> int
  httptest_get(url: string) -> string
  httptest_status() -> int
  httptest_header(name: string) -> string

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.28 Runtime
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  runtime_stats_json() -> string              Runtime stats as JSON
  exit(code: int) -> void                     Exit process
  leak_mark() -> int                          Mark allocation state
  leak_bytes_since(mark: int) -> int          Bytes allocated since mark
  leak_detected(mark: int) -> int             Check for leaks
  alloc_track_reset() -> int                  Reset allocation tracker
  alloc_live_bytes() -> int                   Currently live bytes
  alloc_high_bytes() -> int                   Peak allocated bytes

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.29 Logging
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  log_info(msg: string)                       [timestamp info] msg
  log_warn(msg: string)                       [timestamp warn] msg
  log_error(msg: string)                      [timestamp error] msg
  log_kv(level: string, key: string, val: string)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
24.30 Router & Middleware
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  router_new() -> int                         Create router
  router_group(router: int, prefix: string) -> int  Route group
  router_add(router: int, method: string, pattern: string, handler: string) -> int
  router_match(router: int, req: HttpRequest) -> string
  router_param(router: int, req: HttpRequest, name: string) -> string

  reqctx_new() -> int                         Request context
  reqctx_set(ctx: int, key: string, val: string) -> int
  reqctx_get(ctx: int, key: string) -> string
  middleware_allow_methods(req: HttpRequest, methods: string) -> int

================================================================================
25. COMMON PATTERNS
================================================================================

25.1 HTTP Server
----------------

  fn main() {
      let fd = http_bind(8080)
      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let method = http_method(c)
          let path = http_path(c)

          if str_eq(path, "/health") {
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, "{\"ok\":true}\n")
          } else if str_eq(path, "/api/users") and str_eq(method, "POST") {
              let body = http_body(c)
              let name = json_get_string(body, "name")
              let resp = json_object("created", name)
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 201, resp)
          } else {
              let _ = http_respond(c, 404, "not found\n")
          }
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
  }

25.2 Error Handling Chain
-------------------------

  fn load_config(path: string) -> Result[string, string] {
      if not file_exists(path) {
          return error("config file not found")
      }
      let data = read_file(path)
      if str_len(data) == 0 {
          return error("config file is empty")
      }
      return Ok(data)
  }

  fn start() -> Result[int, string] {
      let cfg = wrap_err(load_config("app.toml"), "startup")?
      let port = parse_int(toml_get_string(cfg, "port"))?
      return Ok(port)
  }

25.3 Concurrent Workers
------------------------

  fn main() {
      let ch = chan_new(100)
      crew t {
          // Spawn 4 workers
          let w1 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w1"))
          let w2 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w2"))
          let w3 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w3"))
          let w4 = t.kick(worker(ch, "w4"))

          // Send work
          for i in range 100 {
              let _ = ch.send(i)
          }
          ch.close()

          // Wait for all workers
          let _ = w1.join()
          let _ = w2.join()
          let _ = w3.join()
          let _ = w4.join()
      }
  }

  fn worker(ch: chan[int], name: string) -> int {
      for v in range ch {
          print(name + ": processing " + format_int(v))
      }
      return 0
  }

25.4 Arena-Scoped Request Processing
-------------------------------------

  fn handle_request(c: int) {
      arena req {
          let mut parts = make([]string, 0, 16)
          let body = http_body(c)
          parts = append(parts, json_get_string(body, "name"))
          parts = append(parts, json_get_string(body, "email"))
          let resp = json_ss("name", parts[0], "email", parts[1])
          let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, resp)
      }
      // All arena memory freed here
      let _ = http_close(c)
  }

25.5 JSON API with Database
----------------------------

  fn main() {
      let db = sql_open_sqlite("/tmp/api.db")
      let _ = sql_exec_plain(db, "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, email TEXT)")

      let fd = http_bind(8080)
      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let path = http_path(c)
          let method = http_method(c)

          if str_eq(method, "POST") and str_eq(path, "/users") {
              let body = http_body(c)
              let name = json_get_string(body, "name")
              let email = json_get_string(body, "email")
              let _ = sql_exec_str4(db, "INSERT INTO users(name,email,'','') VALUES($1,$2,$3,$4)", name, email, "", "")
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 201, json_object("created", name))
          } else if str_eq(method, "GET") and str_has_prefix(path, "/users/") {
              let email = path[7:]
              let name = sql_query_str(db, "SELECT name FROM users WHERE email=$1", email)
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, json_object("name", name))
          } else {
              let _ = http_respond(c, 404, "not found\n")
          }
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
      sql_close(db)
  }

25.6 Session-Based Auth API
---------------------------

  fn main() {
      let sessions = cmap_new()
      let secret = "my-secret-key"
      let fd = http_bind(8080)

      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }
          let path = http_path(c)

          if str_eq(path, "/login") {
              let body = http_body(c)
              let user = json_get_string(body, "user")
              let sid = session_id_new()
              cmap_set(sessions, sid, user)
              let cookie = cookie_make("sid", sid, 86400)
              let _ = http_respond_ct(c, 200, "{\"ok\":true}", "application/json")
          } else if str_eq(path, "/me") {
              let cookie_hdr = http_header(c, "Cookie")
              let sid = cookie_get(cookie_hdr, "sid")
              if cmap_has(sessions, sid) == 1 {
                  let user = cmap_get(sessions, sid)
                  let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, json_object("user", user))
              } else {
                  let _ = http_respond_json(c, 401, "{\"error\":\"unauthorized\"}")
              }
          } else {
              let _ = http_respond(c, 404, "not found\n")
          }
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
  }

================================================================================
25.7 Fan with Crew for Parallel Processing
------------------------------------------

  fn process_batch(items: []int) -> []int {
      let results = fan(items, |item| {
          // Each item processed in parallel
          item * item + 1
      })
      return results
  }

  fn main() {
      let data = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]
      let out = process_batch(data)
      for v in out {
          print_int(v)
      }
  }

25.8 Rate-Limited HTTP Server
-----------------------------

  fn main() {
      let rl = ratelimit_new(100, 1000)   // 100 req/sec
      let fd = http_bind(8080)

      while true {
          let c = http_accept(fd)
          if c < 0 { continue }

          if ratelimit_allow(rl) == 0 {
              let _ = http_respond_json(c, 429, "{\"error\":\"rate limit exceeded\"}")
              let _ = http_close(c)
              continue
          }

          let _ = http_respond_json(c, 200, "{\"ok\":true}")
          let _ = http_close(c)
      }
      ratelimit_free(rl)
  }

25.9 Circuit Breaker for External Calls
----------------------------------------

  fn call_external(cb: CircuitBreaker, url: string) -> string {
      if breaker_allow(cb) == 0 {
          return "{\"error\":\"circuit open\"}"
      }
      let resp = http_get_timeout(url, 5000)
      let status = http_last_status()
      if status >= 200 and status < 300 {
          breaker_success(cb)
          return resp
      }
      breaker_failure(cb)
      return "{\"error\":\"upstream failure\"}"
  }

25.10 Event Loop Server
-----------------------

  fn main() {
      let el = evloop_new()
      let server_fd = nb_listen(8080)
      let _ = evloop_add(el, server_fd, 1)

      while true {
          let n = evloop_wait(el, 1000)
          let mut i = 0
          while i < n {
              let fd = evloop_event_fd(el, i)
              if fd == server_fd {
                  let client = nb_accept(server_fd)
                  if client >= 0 {
                      let _ = evloop_add(el, client, 1)
                  }
              } else {
                  let data = nb_read(fd)
                  let _ = nb_write(fd, "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n\r\nhi\n")
                  let _ = evloop_del(el, fd)
                  let _ = nb_close(fd)
              }
              i = i + 1
          }
      }
      let _ = evloop_close(el)
  }

25.11 Binary Protocol
---------------------

  fn encode_message(msg_type: int, payload: string) -> string {
      let b = buf_pack_new(64)
      buf_write_u8(b, msg_type)
      buf_write_u32be(b, str_len(payload))
      buf_write_bytes(b, payload)
      let wire = buf_to_string(b)
      buf_free(b)
      return wire
  }

  fn decode_message(wire: string) -> string {
      let r = buf_from_string(wire)
      let msg_type = buf_read_u8(r)
      let msg_len = buf_read_u32be(r)
      let payload = buf_read_bytes(r, msg_len)
      buf_free(r)
      return payload
  }

25.12 Game Server Tick Loop
---------------------------

  fn main() {
      let u = game_udp_bind(27015)
      let el = evloop_new()
      let _ = evloop_add(el, game_udp_fd(u), 1)

      let mut tick = tick_now_us()
      let period = 16666       // ~60 Hz in microseconds

      while true {
          let n = evloop_wait(el, 1)
          if n > 0 {
              let data = game_udp_recv(u)
              let peer = game_udp_sender(u)
              let _ = game_udp_send(u, peer, "ack:" + data)
          }
          tick = tick_sleep_us(tick, period)
      }
      game_udp_close(u)
  }

25.13 Consistent Hashing for Distributed Cache
-----------------------------------------------

  fn main() {
      let ring = chash_new(3, 150)     // 3 nodes, 150 vnodes each
      let node = chash_get(ring, "user:42")
      print("route user:42 to node " + format_int(node))

      let new_id = chash_add_node(ring)
      print("added node " + format_int(new_id))

      chash_remove_node(ring, 0)
      print("nodes remaining: " + format_int(chash_node_count(ring)))
      chash_free(ring)
  }

25.14 String Builder Pattern
----------------------------

  fn build_html(items: []string) -> string {
      let mut sb = str_builder()
      builder_write(sb, "<ul>\n")
      for _, item in range items {
          builder_write(sb, "  <li>")
          builder_write(sb, html_escape(item))
          builder_write(sb, "</li>\n")
      }
      builder_write(sb, "</ul>\n")
      return builder_string(sb)
  }

25.15 File Processing
---------------------

  fn process_csv(path: string) -> int {
      let data = read_file(path)
      let lines = str_split(data, "\n")
      let mut count = 0
      for _, line in range lines {
          if str_len(line) == 0 { continue }
          let fields = csv_split_line(line)
          if len(fields) >= 2 {
              print(fields[0] + ": " + fields[1])
              count = count + 1
          }
      }
      return count
  }

25.16 Workspace Layout
----------------------

  # mako.toml (workspace root)
  [workspace]
  members = ["core", "helper", "app"]

  # core/mako.toml
  [package]
  name = "core"

  # helper/mako.toml
  [package]
  name = "helper"
  [dependencies]
  "core" = { path = "../core" }

  # app/mako.toml
  [package]
  name = "app"
  [dependencies]
  "helper" = { path = "../helper" }

  # Commands from workspace root:
  mako check .          # typecheck all members
  mako build .          # build all members with main.mko
  mako test .           # test all members
  mako run -p app       # run specific member

================================================================================
26. KEYWORDS (COMPLETE LIST)
================================================================================

38 reserved words (always keywords, never identifiers):

  actor and arena as break const continue crew default defer else enum extern
  false fan fn for hold if import in interface join kick let match mut not or
  range receive return select share struct timeout true while

NOT keywords (ordinary identifiers / builtins):
  Type names: int, int8, int32, int64, uint64, byte, float, float64, string
  Constructors: Ok, Err, Some, None
  Builtins: len, cap, append, copy, make, has, delete, assert, assert_eq, t_run
  All stdlib functions (print, str_len, etc.)

================================================================================
27. COMPLETE OPERATOR TABLE
================================================================================

  =                   Assignment (never equality)
  == != < > <= >=     Comparison
  + - * / %           Arithmetic
  && || !             Logical (short-circuit)
  and or not          Logical (keyword form, same as above)
  & | ^ &^ << >>      Bitwise
  ^x                  Unary bitwise complement
  |params| { body }   Lambda (leading | is lambda, infix | is bitwise or)

================================================================================
28. TYPE CONVERSION TABLE
================================================================================

  int -> int64:       int64(x)
  int -> int32:       int32(x)
  int -> int8:        int8(x)        range-checked -128..127
  int -> uint64:      uint64(x)      rejects negative
  int -> byte:        byte(x)        range-checked 0..255
  int -> float:       float64(x)
  int -> string:      string(x)      decimal representation
  float -> int:       truncates
  string -> []byte:   bytes(s) or []byte(s)
  []byte -> string:   string(b)

================================================================================
END OF REFERENCE
================================================================================

# For the latest docs, visit https://mako-lang.com
# Full source: https://github.com/loreste/mako
